Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. FCC chairman threatens TV broadcast licenses over news coverage 83 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Library of Short Stories 42 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. A Recursive Algorithm to Render Signed Distance Fields 51 upvotes, 4 comments
  4. Baochip-1x: What it is, why I'm doing it now and how it came about 272 upvotes, 54 comments
  5. SBCL Fibers – Lightweight Cooperative Threads 24 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Bumblebee queens breathe underwater to survive drowning 58 upvotes, 11 comments
  7. Show HN: GrobPaint: Somewhere Between MS Paint and Paint.net 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. Making your JITted Code known: Let me count the ways 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. Marketing for Founders 113 upvotes, 33 comments
  10. Python: The Optimization Ladder 272 upvotes, 98 comments
  11. Learning Creative Coding 37 upvotes, 8 comments
  12. How Kernel Anti-Cheats Work: A Deep Dive into Modern Game Protection 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. Fedora 44 on the Raspberry Pi 5 67 upvotes, 21 comments
  14. Postgres with Builtin File Systems 34 upvotes, 9 comments
  15. Launching the Claude Partner Network 81 upvotes, 29 comments
  16. Federal Surveillance Tech Becomes Mandatory in New Cars by 2027 28 upvotes, 7 comments
  17. Show HN: Ichinichi – One note per day, E2E encrypted, local-first 72 upvotes, 27 comments
  18. Deriving Type Erasure 24 upvotes, 7 comments
  19. Hazardous substances found in all headphones tested by ToxFREE project 71 upvotes, 31 comments
  20. Airbus is preparing two uncrewed combat aircraft 50 upvotes, 20 comments
  21. Tree Search Distillation for Language Models Using PPO 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. Ageless Linux – Software for humans of indeterminate age 290 upvotes, 186 comments
  23. An ode to bzip 96 upvotes, 54 comments
  24. Changes to OpenTTD Distribution on Steam 121 upvotes, 74 comments
  25. Show HN: Han – A Korean programming language written in Rust 108 upvotes, 65 comments
  26. Offloading FFmpeg with Cloudflare 36 upvotes, 18 comments
  27. Montana passes Right to Compute act (2025) 243 upvotes, 206 comments
  28. Starlink militarization and its impact on global strategic stability (2023) 129 upvotes, 168 comments
  29. From Braun T3 to Apple's iPod 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  30. 9 Mothers Defense (YC P26) Is Hiring in Austin 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00am, 15 March 2026. Source here.
